# Fil One Documentation

> Fil One is S3-compatible object storage built for AI agents and data-intensive
> workloads. Backed by the Filecoin network for cryptographically verifiable data
> integrity. No egress fees, no per-request charges, $4.99/TB/month flat rate.
> Use any S3-compatible SDK or tool — point it at `https://eu-west-1.s3.fil.one`
> with region `eu-west-1` and standard Access Key ID / Secret Access Key auth.

Critical integration notes for developers:
- Buckets must be created via the dashboard (app.fil.one) — CreateBucket and
  DeleteBucket via S3 API are not supported and return AccessDenied
- Path-style addressing required: `https://eu-west-1.s3.fil.one/bucket/key`
  (virtual-hosted-style URLs are not supported)
- Authentication: AWS Signature Version 4 (SigV4) — all S3-compatible SDKs
  handle this automatically
- All buckets are private by default; all data is encrypted at rest (AES-256
  SSE-KMS) and in transit (TLS); encryption cannot be disabled
- Single region currently: eu-west-1 (additional regions planned)
- Deletion is permanent and immediate — no recycle bin or soft-delete


---

# Welcome to Fil One

Fil One is an S3-compatible object storage platform designed for teams that need
reliable, cost-effective storage with built-in compliance features. If you've used
AWS S3, you already know how to use Fil One.

## Why Fil One

**S3-compatible from day one.** Use the tools you already know — AWS CLI, boto3,
any S3-compatible SDK. Point your existing code at our endpoint and it works.

**Verifiable data integrity.** Data stored on Fil One is backed by Filecoin, a
distributed storage network where storage providers must continuously prove they
are holding your data through cryptographic proofs. Your data's integrity is
independently verifiable, not just claimed.

**No egress fees.** Download your data as often as you need. There are no data
transfer charges.

**Simple, flat pricing.** $4.99 / TB / month. No per-request charges, no hidden
fees, no tiered pricing.

**30-day free trial.** 1 TB of storage and 2 TB of bandwidth, no credit card
required.


---

# Quickstart

Source: https://docs.fil.one/quickstart

This guide walks you through creating an account, setting up credentials, and
uploading your first object.

## 1. Create your account

1. Go to app.fil.one and sign up with your email or SSO.
2. Verify your email address.
3. No credit card needed — your 30-day free trial starts automatically.

## 2. Create a bucket

From the dashboard, click **Create Bucket**. Give it a name (lowercase, no spaces
— same rules as S3) and choose your settings:

- Region: eu-west-1
- Encryption: Always on (all data is encrypted at rest by default)

You will also see an API key section during bucket creation. You can create a new
API key here or configure permissions for an existing one.

## 3. Upload your first object

Go to **API Keys** in the left menu and click **Create Key**. You'll get two values:

- **Access Key ID** — identifies your account (safe to store in config)
- **Secret Access Key** — authenticates your requests (treat like a password)

Your secret key is shown once. Copy it and store it securely. If you lose it,
you'll need to generate a new key pair.

### AWS CLI

```bash
aws configure set aws_access_key_id YOUR_ACCESS_KEY
aws configure set aws_secret_access_key YOUR_SECRET_KEY
aws configure set default.region eu-west-1

aws s3 cp my-file.txt s3://my-bucket/my-file.txt \
  --endpoint-url https://eu-west-1.s3.fil.one

aws s3 ls s3://my-bucket/ --endpoint-url https://eu-west-1.s3.fil.one
```

### Python (boto3)

```python
import boto3

s3 = boto3.client(
    "s3",
    endpoint_url="https://eu-west-1.s3.fil.one",
    aws_access_key_id="YOUR_ACCESS_KEY",
    aws_secret_access_key="YOUR_SECRET_KEY",
)

s3.upload_file("my-file.txt", "my-bucket", "my-file.txt")

response = s3.list_objects_v2(Bucket="my-bucket")
for obj in response.get("Contents", []):
    print(obj["Key"], obj["Size"])
```

### JavaScript (AWS SDK v3)

```javascript
import { S3Client, PutObjectCommand, ListObjectsV2Command } from "@aws-sdk/client-s3";
import { readFileSync } from "fs";

const client = new S3Client({
  endpoint: "https://eu-west-1.s3.fil.one",
  region: "eu-west-1",
  forcePathStyle: true,
  credentials: {
    accessKeyId: "YOUR_ACCESS_KEY",
    secretAccessKey: "YOUR_SECRET_KEY",
  },
});

await client.send(new PutObjectCommand({
  Bucket: "my-bucket",
  Key: "my-file.txt",
  Body: readFileSync("my-file.txt"),
}));

const { Contents } = await client.send(
  new ListObjectsV2Command({ Bucket: "my-bucket" })
);
Contents?.forEach((obj) => console.log(obj.Key, obj.Size));
```

### Go (aws-sdk-go-v2)

```go
package main

import (
    "context"
    "fmt"
    "os"
    "strings"

    "github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2/aws"
    "github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2/credentials"
    "github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2/service/s3"
)

func main() {
    client := s3.New(s3.Options{
        BaseEndpoint: aws.String("https://eu-west-1.s3.fil.one"),
        Region:       "eu-west-1",
        UsePathStyle: true,
        Credentials: credentials.NewStaticCredentialsProvider(
            os.Getenv("FIL_ACCESS_KEY"),
            os.Getenv("FIL_SECRET_KEY"),
            "",
        ),
    })

    ctx := context.Background()

    client.PutObject(ctx, &s3.PutObjectInput{
        Bucket: aws.String("my-bucket"),
        Key:    aws.String("hello.txt"),
        Body:   strings.NewReader("Hello from Go!"),
    })

    out, _ := client.ListObjectsV2(ctx, &s3.ListObjectsV2Input{
        Bucket: aws.String("my-bucket"),
    })
    for _, obj := range out.Contents {
        fmt.Printf("%s (%d bytes)\n", *obj.Key, *obj.Size)
    }
}
```


---

# Quickstart - AWS CLI

Source: https://docs.fil.one/quickstart/cli

If you have the AWS CLI installed, you can start using Fil One in under 2 minutes.

```bash
# Install
pip install awscli
# or
brew install awscli

# Configure
aws configure
# Enter your Fil One access key and secret key. Set region to eu-west-1.

# Upload
aws s3 cp my-file.txt s3://my-first-bucket/ --endpoint-url https://eu-west-1.s3.fil.one

# List
aws s3 ls s3://my-first-bucket/ --endpoint-url https://eu-west-1.s3.fil.one

# Download
aws s3 cp s3://my-first-bucket/my-file.txt ./downloaded.txt --endpoint-url https://eu-west-1.s3.fil.one
```

Tip: Set up a named profile to avoid repeating --endpoint-url:

```ini
# ~/.aws/config
[profile filone]
endpoint_url = https://eu-west-1.s3.fil.one
region = eu-west-1
```

```ini
# ~/.aws/credentials
[filone]
aws_access_key_id = YOUR_ACCESS_KEY
aws_secret_access_key = YOUR_SECRET_KEY
```

Then use `--profile filone` on every command:

```bash
aws s3 ls --profile filone
```


---

# Buckets

Source: https://docs.fil.one/storage/buckets

Buckets are containers for your objects. Every object in Fil One lives inside a bucket.

## Bucket naming rules

- Between 3 and 63 characters
- Lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens only
- Must start and end with a letter or number
- Globally unique across all Fil One accounts

These are the same naming rules as AWS S3.

## Creating a bucket

Bucket creation and deletion must be done through the Fil One dashboard. The S3
API does not support CreateBucket or DeleteBucket — those operations return
AccessDenied. All other S3 operations work normally via the API.

1. From the dashboard, click **Create Bucket**.
2. Enter a bucket name.
3. Optionally create or configure an API key.
4. Click **Create**.

Every bucket is created with encryption enabled automatically.

## Listing buckets

```bash
aws s3 ls --endpoint-url https://eu-west-1.s3.fil.one
```

```python
response = s3.list_buckets()
for bucket in response["Buckets"]:
    print(bucket["Name"], bucket["CreationDate"])
```

## Deleting a bucket

Bucket deletion is not yet supported. This will be enabled in a future release.

Note: When you delete a bucket and its objects, the data is immediately removed
from your Fil One account. However, the underlying Filecoin storage deals persist
until their natural expiration (up to 180 days). This does not affect your ability
to manage your data in Fil One.


---

# Objects

Source: https://docs.fil.one/storage/objects

Objects are the files you store in Fil One. Each object consists of the file data,
a key (the file path/name), and metadata.

## Uploading objects

```bash
# Upload a single file
aws s3 cp my-file.txt s3://my-bucket/my-file.txt \
  --endpoint-url https://eu-west-1.s3.fil.one

# Upload a directory
aws s3 sync ./local-folder s3://my-bucket/folder/ \
  --endpoint-url https://eu-west-1.s3.fil.one
```

```python
# Upload a file
s3.upload_file("my-file.txt", "my-bucket", "my-file.txt")

# Upload with metadata
s3.put_object(
    Bucket="my-bucket",
    Key="report.pdf",
    Body=open("report.pdf", "rb"),
    ContentType="application/pdf",
)
```

For files larger than 5 GB, use multipart upload.

## Downloading objects

```bash
aws s3 cp s3://my-bucket/my-file.txt ./my-file.txt \
  --endpoint-url https://eu-west-1.s3.fil.one
```

```python
s3.download_file("my-bucket", "my-file.txt", "my-file.txt")
```

## Listing objects

```bash
aws s3 ls s3://my-bucket/ --endpoint-url https://eu-west-1.s3.fil.one
```

Programmatic listing with pagination:

```python
paginator = s3.get_paginator("list_objects_v2")
for page in paginator.paginate(Bucket="my-bucket"):
    for obj in page.get("Contents", []):
        print(obj["Key"], obj["Size"])
```

## Deleting objects

```bash
aws s3 rm s3://my-bucket/my-file.txt \
  --endpoint-url https://eu-west-1.s3.fil.one
```

Deletion is immediate — the object disappears from the dashboard and API
instantly. There is no recycle bin or soft-delete.

## Presigned URLs

Generate a temporary URL that grants time-limited access to a specific object
without requiring API credentials.

```python
# Generate a download URL valid for 1 hour
url = s3.generate_presigned_url(
    "get_object",
    Params={"Bucket": "my-bucket", "Key": "shared-report.pdf"},
    ExpiresIn=3600,
)
```

Presigned URLs work for both GetObject (downloads) and PutObject (uploads).


---

# Multipart Uploads

Source: https://docs.fil.one/storage/multipart-uploads

For objects larger than 5 GB, you must use multipart upload. Recommended for
any file over 100 MB for improved reliability.

## How it works

1. **Initiate** the upload and receive an upload ID.
2. **Upload parts** individually (minimum 5 MB each, except the last part).
3. **Complete** the upload by listing all parts.

## AWS CLI

The AWS CLI handles multipart uploads automatically for large files:

```bash
aws s3 cp large-file.zip s3://my-bucket/large-file.zip \
  --endpoint-url https://eu-west-1.s3.fil.one

# Configure thresholds
aws configure set s3.multipart_threshold 100MB
aws configure set s3.multipart_chunksize 50MB
```

## Python (boto3)

```python
from boto3.s3.transfer import TransferConfig

config = TransferConfig(
    multipart_threshold=100 * 1024 * 1024,  # 100 MB
    multipart_chunksize=50 * 1024 * 1024,   # 50 MB
    max_concurrency=10,
)

s3.upload_file("large-file.zip", "my-bucket", "large-file.zip", Config=config)
```

## Limits

| Parameter | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Maximum object size | 5 TB |
| Maximum parts | 10,000 |
| Minimum part size | 5 MB (except last part) |
| Maximum part size | 5 GB |

## Cleaning up incomplete uploads

```bash
aws s3api list-multipart-uploads \
  --bucket my-bucket \
  --endpoint-url https://eu-west-1.s3.fil.one

aws s3api abort-multipart-upload \
  --bucket my-bucket \
  --key large-file.zip \
  --upload-id YOUR_UPLOAD_ID \
  --endpoint-url https://eu-west-1.s3.fil.one
```


---

# Authentication

Source: https://docs.fil.one/security/authentication

All API requests to Fil One must be authenticated using AWS Signature Version 4
(SigV4). Any S3-compatible SDK or tool handles this automatically.

## API keys

| Component | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| Access Key ID | Identifies your account. Included in request headers. Format: FHXXXXXXXXXXXXXXXX |
| Secret Access Key | Signs your requests. Never sent over the wire. |

Your secret key is displayed once at creation time. If you lose it, you must
create a new key pair.

## Configuring your client

### AWS CLI

```bash
aws configure
# AWS Access Key ID: YOUR_ACCESS_KEY
# AWS Secret Access Key: YOUR_SECRET_KEY
# Default region name: eu-west-1
```

Named profile (recommended):

```ini
# ~/.aws/config
[profile filone]
endpoint_url = https://eu-west-1.s3.fil.one
region = eu-west-1

# ~/.aws/credentials
[filone]
aws_access_key_id = YOUR_ACCESS_KEY
aws_secret_access_key = YOUR_SECRET_KEY
```

### Python (boto3)

```python
import boto3
import os

s3 = boto3.client(
    "s3",
    endpoint_url="https://eu-west-1.s3.fil.one",
    aws_access_key_id=os.environ["FIL_ACCESS_KEY"],
    aws_secret_access_key=os.environ["FIL_SECRET_KEY"],
    region_name="eu-west-1",
)
```

### JavaScript (AWS SDK v3)

```javascript
import { S3Client } from "@aws-sdk/client-s3";

const client = new S3Client({
  endpoint: "https://eu-west-1.s3.fil.one",
  region: "eu-west-1",
  forcePathStyle: true,
  credentials: {
    accessKeyId: process.env.FIL_ACCESS_KEY,
    secretAccessKey: process.env.FIL_SECRET_KEY,
  },
});
```

### Go

```go
func newClient() *s3.Client {
    return s3.New(s3.Options{
        BaseEndpoint: aws.String("https://eu-west-1.s3.fil.one"),
        Region:       "eu-west-1",
        UsePathStyle: true,
        Credentials: credentials.NewStaticCredentialsProvider(
            os.Getenv("FIL_ACCESS_KEY"),
            os.Getenv("FIL_SECRET_KEY"),
            "",
        ),
    })
}
```

## Security best practices

- Do not hardcode credentials in source code. Use environment variables or a
  secrets manager.
- Create separate keys for different environments.
- Rotate keys periodically.
- Revoke keys immediately if compromised.


---

# API Keys

Source: https://docs.fil.one/security/api-keys

API keys authenticate your requests to the Fil One S3 API. Each key consists of
an Access Key ID (public identifier) and a Secret Access Key (used to sign requests).

## Creating an API key

1. Go to **API Keys** in the left menu of the dashboard.
2. Click **Create Key**.
3. Give the key a descriptive name (e.g., production-backend, ci-pipeline).
4. Set permissions and scope.
5. Optionally set an expiration date.
6. Click **Create**.
7. **Copy the Secret Access Key immediately.** It is shown only once.

## Key permissions

| Permission | What it allows |
|------------|----------------|
| Read | GetObject, HeadObject |
| Write | PutObject, CopyObject, multipart upload operations |
| List | ListBuckets, ListObjectsV2 |
| Delete | DeleteObject |

## Bucket scope

By default, a key has access to all buckets in your account. You can restrict a
key to specific buckets. This limits the blast radius of a compromised key.

## Key expiration

You can set an expiration date when creating a key. After expiration, the key is
automatically revoked and requests fail with 403 AccessDenied.

## Security best practices

- Use separate keys per environment (development, staging, production).
- Use the minimum permissions required.
- Scope keys to specific buckets when possible.
- Set expiration dates on temporary keys.
- Rotate keys periodically.
- Revoke immediately if compromised.
- Never hardcode credentials in source code.


---

# Encryption

Source: https://docs.fil.one/security/encryption

Fil One encrypts all data by default. There is nothing to configure.

## Encryption at rest

All objects are encrypted using AES-256 via SSE-KMS (Server-Side Encryption with
Key Management Service). Applied automatically on write, decrypted automatically
on read. Applies to all objects in all buckets with no exceptions.

## Encryption in transit

All connections to https://s3.fil.one use TLS. HTTP connections are rejected.
Applies to all API operations including uploads, downloads, presigned URLs,
and metadata requests.

## Client-side encryption

Optional. If your requirements call for client-side encryption, you can use the
encryption utilities in the AWS SDKs. Fil One stores whatever bytes you send.
Client-side encryption is in addition to the server-side encryption that Fil One
applies automatically.


---

# Pricing

Source: https://docs.fil.one/billing/pricing

Fil One is priced at **$4.99 / TB / month**.

No egress fees, no per-request charges, no hidden costs.


---

# Trial & Billing

Source: https://docs.fil.one/billing/trial

## Free trial

Every new account starts with a 30-day free trial. No credit card required.

| Included | Amount |
|----------|--------|
| Storage | 1 TB (1,024 GB) |
| Egress bandwidth | 2 TB |
| API operations | Unlimited |
| Duration | 30 days from account creation |

## What happens when the trial ends

| Phase | Days | What you can do |
|-------|------|-----------------|
| Active trial | 0-30 | Full access. |
| Grace period | 31-37 | Read-only. Download your data and upgrade. |
| Expired | 38+ | Account closed. All data permanently deleted. |

You can upgrade to a paid plan at any time during the trial or grace period.

## Paid plan pricing

| Component | Price |
|-----------|-------|
| Storage | $4.99 / TB / month |
| Egress bandwidth | No charge |
| API operations | Included |

No minimum commitments. Billed based on average daily usage.


---

# API Reference Overview

Source: https://docs.fil.one/reference/overview

## Base endpoint

```
https://eu-west-1.s3.fil.one
```

All requests must use HTTPS and authenticate with AWS Signature Version 4 (SigV4).

## Request format

Use path-style addressing:

```
https://eu-west-1.s3.fil.one/my-bucket/my-key
```

Virtual-hosted-style URLs (https://my-bucket.s3.fil.one/my-key) are not supported.
Configure your S3 client to use path-style requests:
- boto3: no special config needed (path-style is default for custom endpoints)
- AWS SDK JS v3: set `forcePathStyle: true`
- Go aws-sdk-go-v2: set `UsePathStyle: true`

## Response format

Standard S3 conventions — XML for list and error responses, raw bytes for object
data. Standard S3 headers included (x-amz-request-id, ETag, Content-Length, etc.).


---

# S3 Compatibility

Source: https://docs.fil.one/reference/s3-compatibility

Fil One provides an S3-compatible API. Point your SDK or CLI at the Fil One
endpoint and authenticate with your API keys.

## Connection parameters

| Parameter | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Endpoint | https://eu-west-1.s3.fil.one |
| Region | eu-west-1 |
| Signature version | SigV4 |
| Path style | Required |
| Virtual-hosted style | Not supported |

## Supported operations

### Bucket operations

| Operation | Status | Notes |
|-----------|--------|-------|
| CreateBucket | Not supported | Buckets must be created via the dashboard. |
| DeleteBucket | Not supported | Bucket deletion not yet available. |
| ListBuckets | Supported | |
| HeadBucket | Supported | |

### Object operations

| Operation | Status | Notes |
|-----------|--------|-------|
| PutObject | Supported | Max 5 TB (use multipart for > 5 GB). |
| GetObject | Supported | |
| DeleteObject | Supported | |
| HeadObject | Supported | |
| ListObjectsV2 | Supported | |
| CopyObject | Supported | |

### Multipart upload

| Operation | Status |
|-----------|--------|
| CreateMultipartUpload | Supported |
| UploadPart | Supported |
| CompleteMultipartUpload | Supported |
| AbortMultipartUpload | Supported |

### Presigned URLs

| Operation | Status |
|-----------|--------|
| Presigned GetObject | Supported |
| Presigned PutObject | Supported |

### Not yet supported

Bucket policies / ACLs, lifecycle rules, S3 Select, S3 Inventory, S3 Batch
Operations, IAM policies, public access settings.

## Differences from AWS S3

| Behavior | AWS S3 | Fil One |
|----------|--------|---------|
| Bucket management | CreateBucket/DeleteBucket via API | Dashboard only |
| IAM policies | Full support | Not supported (planned) |
| Public access | Configurable ACLs | Not supported; all access requires auth |
| Lifecycle rules | Supported | Not supported (planned) |
| Storage classes | Multiple | Single class |
| Regions | 30+ | eu-west-1 (more planned) |


---

# Error Reference

Source: https://docs.fil.one/reference/errors

Fil One returns standard S3 error responses in XML format.

## Error response format

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<Error>
  <Code>NoSuchBucket</Code>
  <Message>The specified bucket does not exist.</Message>
  <BucketName>my-bucket</BucketName>
  <RequestId>abc123def456</RequestId>
</Error>
```

## Common error codes

### Client errors (4xx)

| Code | HTTP | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| AccessDenied | 403 | No permission. Also returned for CreateBucket/DeleteBucket (not supported). |
| BucketAlreadyExists | 409 | Bucket name already taken. |
| InvalidAccessKeyId | 403 | Key does not exist or has been revoked. |
| InvalidBucketName | 400 | Name does not meet naming requirements. |
| NoSuchBucket | 404 | Bucket does not exist. |
| NoSuchKey | 404 | Object does not exist. |
| SignatureDoesNotMatch | 403 | Request signature mismatch. Check secret key and signing method. |
| TooManyBuckets | 400 | Account bucket limit reached. |

### Server errors (5xx)

| Code | HTTP | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| InternalError | 500 | Retry the request. |
| ServiceUnavailable | 503 | Temporarily unavailable. Check Retry-After header. |
| SlowDown | 503 | Too many requests. Implement exponential backoff. |

## Troubleshooting

**SignatureDoesNotMatch**: Re-copy your secret key from the dashboard. Ensure your
system clock is accurate (SigV4 requires clocks within 15 minutes of server time).
Verify region is eu-west-1.

**ServiceUnavailable (503)**: Transient. Retry with exponential backoff.


---

# SDKs & Tools Overview

Source: https://docs.fil.one/sdk/overview

Fil One works with any tool or library that supports the S3 API.

## Connection details

| Parameter | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Endpoint | https://eu-west-1.s3.fil.one |
| Region | eu-west-1 |
| Credentials | Access Key ID + Secret Access Key from dashboard |

## Recommended tools

| Tool | Platform | Install |
|------|----------|---------|
| AWS CLI | Command line | pip install awscli |
| boto3 | Python | pip install boto3 |
| AWS SDK v3 | JavaScript/TypeScript | npm install @aws-sdk/client-s3 |
| aws-sdk-go-v2 | Go | go get github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2/service/s3 |
| rclone | Multi-platform CLI | rclone.org/install |
| Cyberduck | macOS/Windows GUI | cyberduck.io |

## Third-party tools

- **rclone**: Sync files with `rclone config` using S3 provider type.
- **Cyberduck**: Add S3 connection with the Fil One endpoint.
- **s3cmd**: Configure with `s3cmd --configure`, set host to eu-west-1.s3.fil.one.
- **MinIO Client**: `mc alias set filone https://eu-west-1.s3.fil.one YOUR_KEY YOUR_SECRET`


---

# Python (boto3)

Source: https://docs.fil.one/sdk/python

```bash
pip install boto3
```

## Client configuration

```python
import boto3
import os

s3 = boto3.client(
    "s3",
    endpoint_url="https://eu-west-1.s3.fil.one",
    aws_access_key_id=os.environ["FIL_ACCESS_KEY"],
    aws_secret_access_key=os.environ["FIL_SECRET_KEY"],
    region_name="eu-west-1",
)
```

## Core operations

```python
# Upload
s3.upload_file("report.pdf", "my-bucket", "reports/report.pdf")
s3.put_object(Bucket="my-bucket", Key="data/file.json",
              Body=b'{"key": "value"}', ContentType="application/json")

# Download
s3.download_file("my-bucket", "reports/report.pdf", "local-report.pdf")

# List (with pagination)
paginator = s3.get_paginator("list_objects_v2")
for page in paginator.paginate(Bucket="my-bucket"):
    for obj in page.get("Contents", []):
        print(obj["Key"], obj["Size"])

# Delete
s3.delete_object(Bucket="my-bucket", Key="reports/report.pdf")
```

## Multipart uploads

```python
from boto3.s3.transfer import TransferConfig

config = TransferConfig(
    multipart_threshold=100 * 1024 * 1024,  # 100 MB
    multipart_chunksize=50 * 1024 * 1024,   # 50 MB
    max_concurrency=10,
)
s3.upload_file("large-file.zip", "my-bucket", "large-file.zip", Config=config)
```

## Presigned URLs

```python
download_url = s3.generate_presigned_url(
    "get_object",
    Params={"Bucket": "my-bucket", "Key": "reports/report.pdf"},
    ExpiresIn=3600,
)

upload_url = s3.generate_presigned_url(
    "put_object",
    Params={"Bucket": "my-bucket", "Key": "uploads/new-file.txt"},
    ExpiresIn=900,
)
```

## Error handling

```python
from botocore.exceptions import ClientError

try:
    s3.get_object(Bucket="my-bucket", Key="missing-file.txt")
except ClientError as e:
    code = e.response["Error"]["Code"]
    if code == "NoSuchKey":
        print("Object not found")
    elif code == "AccessDenied":
        print("Access denied — check key permissions")
    else:
        raise
```


---

# JavaScript (AWS SDK v3)

Source: https://docs.fil.one/sdk/javascript

```bash
npm install @aws-sdk/client-s3 @aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner
```

## Client configuration

```javascript
import { S3Client } from "@aws-sdk/client-s3";

const s3 = new S3Client({
  endpoint: "https://eu-west-1.s3.fil.one",
  region: "eu-west-1",
  forcePathStyle: true,  // required
  credentials: {
    accessKeyId: process.env.FIL_ACCESS_KEY,
    secretAccessKey: process.env.FIL_SECRET_KEY,
  },
});
```

## Core operations

```javascript
import {
  PutObjectCommand, GetObjectCommand, ListObjectsV2Command,
  DeleteObjectCommand
} from "@aws-sdk/client-s3";
import { readFileSync, writeFileSync } from "fs";

// Upload
await s3.send(new PutObjectCommand({
  Bucket: "my-bucket", Key: "data/file.json",
  Body: JSON.stringify({ key: "value" }),
  ContentType: "application/json",
}));

// Download (streaming)
const response = await s3.send(new GetObjectCommand({
  Bucket: "my-bucket", Key: "reports/report.pdf",
}));
const chunks = [];
for await (const chunk of response.Body) chunks.push(chunk);
writeFileSync("local-report.pdf", Buffer.concat(chunks));

// List (with pagination)
let continuationToken;
do {
  const response = await s3.send(new ListObjectsV2Command({
    Bucket: "my-bucket", ContinuationToken: continuationToken,
  }));
  for (const obj of response.Contents ?? []) console.log(obj.Key, obj.Size);
  continuationToken = response.NextContinuationToken;
} while (continuationToken);

// Delete
await s3.send(new DeleteObjectCommand({ Bucket: "my-bucket", Key: "file.txt" }));
```

## Presigned URLs

```javascript
import { getSignedUrl } from "@aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner";

const downloadUrl = await getSignedUrl(
  s3, new GetObjectCommand({ Bucket: "my-bucket", Key: "reports/report.pdf" }),
  { expiresIn: 3600 }
);

const uploadUrl = await getSignedUrl(
  s3, new PutObjectCommand({ Bucket: "my-bucket", Key: "uploads/new-file.txt" }),
  { expiresIn: 900 }
);
```

## Error handling

```javascript
import { S3ServiceException } from "@aws-sdk/client-s3";

try {
  await s3.send(new GetObjectCommand({ Bucket: "my-bucket", Key: "missing.txt" }));
} catch (err) {
  if (err instanceof S3ServiceException) {
    console.error(err.name, err.message); // e.g. "NoSuchKey"
  } else {
    throw err;
  }
}
```


---

# Go (aws-sdk-go-v2)

Source: https://docs.fil.one/sdk/go

```bash
go get github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2/aws
go get github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2/credentials
go get github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2/service/s3
```

## Client configuration

```go
func newClient() *s3.Client {
    return s3.New(s3.Options{
        BaseEndpoint: aws.String("https://eu-west-1.s3.fil.one"),
        Region:       "eu-west-1",
        UsePathStyle: true,  // required
        Credentials: credentials.NewStaticCredentialsProvider(
            os.Getenv("FIL_ACCESS_KEY"),
            os.Getenv("FIL_SECRET_KEY"),
            "",
        ),
    })
}
```

## Core operations

```go
// Upload bytes
client.PutObject(ctx, &s3.PutObjectInput{
    Bucket: aws.String("my-bucket"), Key: aws.String("data/file.json"),
    Body: bytes.NewReader([]byte(`{"key":"value"}`)),
    ContentType: aws.String("application/json"),
})

// Upload file
f, _ := os.Open("report.pdf")
defer f.Close()
client.PutObject(ctx, &s3.PutObjectInput{
    Bucket: aws.String("my-bucket"), Key: aws.String("reports/report.pdf"),
    Body: f, ContentType: aws.String("application/pdf"),
})

// Download
result, _ := client.GetObject(ctx, &s3.GetObjectInput{
    Bucket: aws.String("my-bucket"), Key: aws.String("reports/report.pdf"),
})
defer result.Body.Close()
out, _ := os.Create("local-report.pdf")
io.Copy(out, result.Body)

// List (paginated)
paginator := s3.NewListObjectsV2Paginator(client, &s3.ListObjectsV2Input{
    Bucket: aws.String("my-bucket"),
})
for paginator.HasMorePages() {
    page, _ := paginator.NextPage(ctx)
    for _, obj := range page.Contents {
        fmt.Println(*obj.Key, obj.Size)
    }
}

// Delete
client.DeleteObject(ctx, &s3.DeleteObjectInput{
    Bucket: aws.String("my-bucket"), Key: aws.String("reports/report.pdf"),
})
```

## Presigned URLs

```go
presigner := s3.NewPresignClient(client)

downloadReq, _ := presigner.PresignGetObject(ctx, &s3.GetObjectInput{
    Bucket: aws.String("my-bucket"), Key: aws.String("reports/report.pdf"),
}, s3.WithPresignExpires(time.Hour))

uploadReq, _ := presigner.PresignPutObject(ctx, &s3.PutObjectInput{
    Bucket: aws.String("my-bucket"), Key: aws.String("uploads/new-file.txt"),
}, s3.WithPresignExpires(15*time.Minute))
```


---

# FAQ

Source: https://docs.fil.one/faq

**Is Fil One S3-compatible?**
Yes. Any application, SDK, or tool that works with AWS S3 works with Fil One by
changing the endpoint URL to https://eu-west-1.s3.fil.one.

**Do I need to know anything about blockchain or cryptocurrency?**
No. Fil One is a standard cloud storage service. You sign up with an email, store
data using familiar S3 tools, and pay with a credit card.

**Where is my data stored?**
On a distributed network of storage providers. All data is encrypted at rest.

**What happens when I delete an object?**
The object disappears from your account immediately. There is no recycle bin or undo.

**Can I migrate from AWS S3?**
Yes. Use aws s3 sync, rclone, or any S3-compatible transfer utility pointed at
the Fil One endpoint.

**Do you charge for egress?**
No. There are no egress or data transfer fees.

**What is included in the free trial?**
30-day trial with 1 TB storage, 2 TB egress, unlimited API operations. No credit
card required.

**Can I use Fil One for backups?**
Yes. Works with Veeam, Commvault, Restic, Duplicati, and any S3-compatible backup tool.

**How does Fil One verify data integrity?**
Built on Filecoin, where storage providers must continuously submit cryptographic
proofs (Proof of Replication and Proof of Spacetime) to prove they hold your data.
Each object is identified by a content identifier (CID), a cryptographic fingerprint.
If the data changes, the CID changes.

**What happens to my data if I delete it?**
Immediately removed from your Fil One account and no longer accessible. The
underlying Filecoin storage deals persist until natural expiration (up to 180 days),
but the data is inaccessible from the moment of deletion.

**Can multiple users access the same account?**
Currently accounts are single-user. Multi-user organization accounts with RBAC
are planned. You can create multiple scoped API keys to share limited access.


---

# Limits & Quotas

Source: https://docs.fil.one/limits

## Account limits

| Limit | Trial | Paid |
|-------|-------|------|
| Storage | 1 TB | No limit |
| Egress bandwidth | 2 TB / month | No limit |
| API operations | Unlimited | Unlimited |
| Buckets per account | 100 | 1,000 |

## Object limits

| Limit | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Maximum object size | 5 TB |
| Maximum object size (single PUT) | 5 GB |
| Maximum parts per multipart upload | 10,000 |
| Minimum part size (multipart) | 5 MB |
| Maximum part size (multipart) | 5 GB |
| Maximum object key length | 263 bytes |
| Maximum metadata size per object | 2 KB |

## API rate limits

Rate limits are enforced by the underlying storage gateway. If you encounter
SlowDown or ServiceUnavailable responses, use exponential backoff. Contact
support if you are consistently hitting limits at scale.

## Naming constraints

**Bucket names:** 3-63 characters, lowercase letters/numbers/hyphens, must start
and end with a letter or number, globally unique.

**Object keys:** Up to 263 bytes (UTF-8). Avoid control characters and characters
requiring special URL encoding for best compatibility.
